subject: [P1] api-gateway-prod 5xx rate spike (us-east)
bodyPreview: Service: api-gateway-prod. Region: us-east. 5xx rate jumped from 0.04% to 7.1% at 02:14 UTC. Incident #INC-INC-30099 opened.
body:
Incident INC-INC-30099 opened at 2026-06-04T02:14:17Z

Service:   api-gateway-prod
Region:    us-east
Metric:    http_5xx_rate
Threshold: > 1.0% over 5min
Observed:  7.1% (peak 9.4%)

Paged: Theo Nakamura (primary), Elena Morales (secondary).

View in ServiceNow: https://servicenow.example.com/incident/INC-30099

subject: Re: [P1] api-gateway-prod 5xx rate spike (us-east)
bodyPreview: Found it. Yesterday's gateway config push had a regex that matches our new /v2/ routes incorrectly — falling through to a default 503. Rolling back now.
body:
Aisha,

Found it. Yesterday's gateway config push (PR #2841, deployed 21:40 UTC) had a route regex that matches our new /v2/ paths incorrectly and falls through to a default 503.

Rolling back to the previous config now. ETA recovery 5 minutes.

Will follow with a proper RCA + post-mortem on the wiki tomorrow.

— Theo

subject: Re: [P1] api-gateway-prod 5xx rate spike (us-east)
bodyPreview: Marcus, FYI — Theo caught it and rolled back, fully recovered by 02:47. Will share the post-mortem on Friday. We'll tighten staging coverage for /v2/ routes before the next push.
body:
Marcus,

FYI — Theo caught it and rolled back, fully recovered by 02:47 UTC. Customer impact ~33 minutes; ServiceNow ticket INC-30099 has the timeline.

Will share the post-mortem on Friday. Two follow-ups:
  1. Tighten staging coverage for /v2/ routes before the next gateway push
  2. Add an alarm on route-fallthrough specifically (not just 5xx aggregate)

No customer comms needed — none of the affected traffic hit external partners.

— Aisha

subject: May 2026 close — variance commentary requested by Mon 9 Jun
bodyPreview: Team, May close kicks off Monday. Please drop variance commentary for any cost centre running > ±5% vs prior year into the shared workbook by EOD Monday Cleveland time.
body:
Team,

May 2026 close kicks off Monday 8 June. To stay on the 5-day close timeline I need variance commentary by EOD Mon 9 Jun Cleveland time for any cost centre running > ±5% vs prior year.

The variance dashboard refreshes overnight: https://sap.example.com/variance-may-2026

What I need from each owner:
  • Driver: volume / price / mix / one-off?
  • Outlook: is the run-rate expected to continue?
  • Action: any planned remediation, or recommended forecast adjustment?

Hiroshi will send a per-owner reminder Wednesday for anyone outstanding.

Thanks,
Sofia

subject: May close — variance reminder for CC-0011 (Platform Engineering)
bodyPreview: Aisha, friendly nudge — CC-0011 is running +14% on Software & Subscriptions vs PY. Need a one-paragraph driver + outlook for Sofia by Mon EOD.
body:
Aisha,

Friendly nudge — your cost centre CC-0011 Platform Engineering is running +14.3% on Software & Subscriptions (GL 6400) vs prior year YTD.

For Sofia's pack I need a one-paragraph note covering:
  • Driver: which subscription drove the increase?
  • Outlook: is the new run-rate expected to continue or is this a one-off catch-up?
  • Any planned change?

Workbook: https://sharepoint.example.com/finance/close/may-2026/variance-workbook.xlsx — your row is CC-0011.

Deadline EOD Mon 9 Jun.

Thanks,
Hiroshi

subject: New hire — Priya Subramaniam — start date 2026-06-22
bodyPreview: Aisha, confirming Priya Subramaniam joins your team as Senior SRE on 22 June. She'll fill REQ-2009 (POS-2103). I've kicked off the People-team onboarding flow.
body:
Aisha,

Confirming Priya Subramaniam joins your team as Senior Site Reliability Engineer on Monday 22 June, filling requisition REQ-2009 (POS-2103, US-remote, regular FT).

People onboarding kicked off this morning:
  • Workday pre-hire record created (employee number will be assigned on day 1)
  • Offer letter countersigned 2026-05-29
  • Background check cleared 2026-06-01

Wesley's team will provision:
  • M365 mailbox + Teams (priya.subramaniam@olympus.example.com)
  • Laptop shipped to home address — expected delivery 2026-06-20
  • Entra access groups: eng-platform, github-org, vpn-corp

Action for you: please book a 30-min welcome 1:1 for her first day and forward the team welcome email.

Thanks,
Beatrix

subject: Parental leave — coverage notes (handover doc attached)
bodyPreview: Aisha, my parental leave starts Monday. I've put my full handover in the shared OneDrive — link below. Theo is taking the on-call rotation, Elena owns the cache migration.
body:
Aisha,

My parental leave starts Monday 8 June and runs through 15 July. Full handover doc lives here:
https://sharepoint.example.com/eng/platform/owen-handover-2026-q2.docx

Summary of who's covering what:
  • On-call rotation — Theo Nakamura (he's already on the calendar)
  • Redis cluster migration — Elena Morales (mid-way through, runbook in handover)
  • Quarterly capacity review — please re-assign; I'd suggest Maya (she sat in on the last one)

My phone will be off. PagerDuty page Theo for anything urgent. For anything that can wait, queue it for me on the team board with a "post-leave" tag.

See you in July,
Owen

subject: Sentinel Observability — June 2026 licence renewal — $42,000
bodyPreview: Per agreement SE-2025-118, the June 2026 licence quarter is $42,000. Invoice will arrive on or before 2026-06-15. Net-30 from invoice date.
body:
Hello,

Per master services agreement SE-2025-118, the June 2026 licence quarter (Apr-Jun) is $42,000.00 USD.

Formal invoice INV-Q2-2026-1024 will be issued on or before 2026-06-15 (we close billing on the 14th). Payment terms net-30 from invoice date.

No action needed at this time — flagging early so AP can accrue if helpful.

Best,
Claudia Fischer
Senior AR Lead, Sentinel Observability Inc

body:

— AP

subject: IT Service Desk — weekly stats wk22 — VIP queue backlog
bodyPreview: Wk22 numbers: 312 tickets opened, 298 closed. VIP queue is the concern — 7 open vs avg 2; biggest contributor is the Munich VPN rollout.
body:
Wesley,

Wk22 service-desk stats (period 2026-05-25 to 2026-05-31):
  Opened:   312
  Closed:   298
  Open:      54 (steady)
  VIP open:   7  ⚠️  (4-week average is 2)

VIP backlog driver: Munich VPN rollout snagged for Maya Patel + Jamal Carter + two execs travelling there next week. Lucia is on it; ETA Friday.

Nothing else outside SLA. Full report attached.

— Frank

subject: Q2 board prep — pre-read due Friday
bodyPreview: Team — Q2 board is 18 June. Pre-read packets due in OneDrive by EOD Friday 12 June. Same template as Q1; Sofia is collating.
body:
Team,

Q2 board is Tuesday 18 June, 09:00 ET, Cleveland HQ + Teams.

Pre-read sections due in OneDrive by EOD Friday 12 June Cleveland time, same template as Q1. Sofia is collating.

My three asks for this board:
  1. Honest read on the manufacturing capacity programme — where is it stuck?
  2. May close — narrative on the variances Sofia flagged, not a recital of numbers
  3. People — Beatrix to lead on retention + 2026 hiring revisions

If you want airtime on anything not on the agenda, tell me by Monday.

Diana

subject: Re: May close — variance reminder for CC-0011
bodyPreview: Hiroshi — May variance commentary for CC-0011 below. Driver is the Sentinel Observability uplift; planned and continues. Will accrue $42k for June separately.
body:
Hiroshi (cc Sofia),

May variance for CC-0011 Platform Engineering — GL 6400 Software & Subscriptions, +14.3% vs PY YTD ($1.82M vs $1.59M):

Driver: Annual licence uplift on Sentinel Observability moved from $128k/qtr to $168k/qtr starting Q1 (a planned change, signed off in 2026 budget — agreement SE-2025-118). Trellis Cloud is also +9% on compute, consistent with traffic growth.

Outlook: Run-rate continues; full-year forecast already reflects this and we're tracking within +1.2% of plan at the cost-centre level.

Action: No remediation required. I'll accrue $42k for the June Sentinel quarter (Apr-Jun) before close, given their invoice doesn't land until 15 June.

Let me know if you need more depth for Sofia's pack.

— Aisha
